# @zakkster/lite-headless/slider

createSlider(options) -> SliderHandle

Headless slider/range primitive. The first form-control in the package;
NOT an overlay (no portal, no positioner, no dismiss layer).

## Value shape

Value is always an array. Length is fixed at construction by the initial
value array's length:

- `[50]`               -- single-thumb slider
- `[25, 75]`           -- two-thumb range slider
- `[10, 40, 70]`       -- multi-thumb (no upper bound)

## Options

- value: WriteSignal<number[]>         -- external controlled mode
- defaultValue: number[]               -- uncontrolled; sets thumb count
- onValueChange: (next, reason) => void

- min: number = 0
- max: number = 100                    -- must be > min
- step: number = 1                     -- must be > 0
- largeStep: number = step * 10        -- PageUp/PageDown + Shift+Arrow

- orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" = "horizontal"
- inverted: boolean = false            -- reverses the visible axis
- disabled: boolean = false

- minStepsBetweenThumbs: number = 0    -- crossing constraint for multi-thumb
                                          0  = thumbs can touch, not cross
                                          N  = must keep N*step apart
                                          -Infinity = thumbs can cross freely

## Returned

{
    value: () => number[],             -- read current values (snapshot copy)
    setValue(values, reason?),         -- write; accepts array or single number
    isDisabled() => boolean,           -- current disabled state
    setDisabled(flag),                 -- runtime enable/disable; cancels any
                                       --   in-flight drag, repaints aria-disabled,
                                       --   data-disabled, and tabindex on every
                                       --   attached thumb + the track
    min, max, step, largeStep, orientation, inverted, thumbCount,
    attachTrack(el),                   -- track element (clicks jump nearest thumb)
    attachRange(el),                   -- range fill element (CSS vars set on it)
    attachThumb(el, index),            -- thumb element at the given index
    attachLabel(el),                   -- wires aria-labelledby on all thumbs
    destroy(),
    destroyed
}

## Positioning contract (consumer CSS)

The primitive writes CSS custom properties; consumer styles them. No inline
style.left or style.transform is written by the primitive.

On each thumb:
  --lh-thumb-pct        0-100, inverted-aware (the visible-axis position)
  data-value            the snapped value
  data-percentage       same as --lh-thumb-pct but with 2dp formatting
  data-thumb-index      "0", "1", ...

On the range fill:
  --lh-range-start      0-100, inverted-aware
  --lh-range-end        0-100, inverted-aware

On the track:
  data-orientation      "horizontal" | "vertical"
  data-dragging         present while a drag is in progress

Example consumer CSS:

    .lh-track  { position: relative; height: 4px; background: #333; }
    .lh-range  {
        position: absolute;
        left:  calc(var(--lh-range-start) * 1%);
        width: calc((var(--lh-range-end) - var(--lh-range-start)) * 1%);
        height: 100%;
        background: var(--accent);
    }
    .lh-thumb  {
        position: absolute;
        left: calc(var(--lh-thumb-pct) * 1%);
        transform: translateX(-50%);
        width: 16px; height: 16px; border-radius: 50%;
        background: #fff;
    }

## Keyboard model

On a focused thumb:
  ArrowRight / ArrowUp        +step
  ArrowLeft / ArrowDown       -step
  Shift+Arrow                 ±largeStep
  PageUp                      +largeStep
  PageDown                    -largeStep
  Home                        snap to min
  End                         snap to max

Keyboard semantics are about VALUE direction, not screen direction. ArrowUp
always increases the value regardless of orientation or inversion -- this
matches what assistive tech expects.

## Pointer drag

- Pointerdown on a thumb starts a drag.
- Pointerdown on the track (not a thumb) moves the nearest thumb to the
  click position AND starts a drag from there (press-and-drag from anywhere).
- During a drag, document-level pointermove and pointerup listeners are
  installed; they're removed on pointerup or pointercancel.
- happy-dom does not lay out elements; getBoundingClientRect returns zeros.
  Real-browser drag works correctly because the consumer's CSS gives the
  track real dimensions.

## Disabled

Pointer events are ignored. Keyboard events are ignored. Thumbs have
tabindex="-1" (not focusable). aria-disabled="true" is set on each thumb.

## ARIA

- role="slider"               on each thumb
- aria-valuenow               on each thumb (reflects the current value)
- aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax on each thumb
- aria-orientation            on each thumb
- aria-labelledby             on each thumb when attachLabel(el) is called
- aria-disabled               on each thumb when disabled

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
